source_jobs.title: Lift Operator- Part Time
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LITERATI

Literati is a modern children’s book company focused on building reading skills so kids can grow to be their best selves. We make it easy to find books kids love through school book fairs and book clubs using a unique mix of expert curation and data science. We’re on a mission to help parents ignite a love of learning and a love of life.

As a Warehouse Associate focused on the picking and packing of customer orders, you’ll be an essential part of bringing book fair magic to life and ensuring timely shipments to our customers. You’ll be responsible for picking and packing fixtures, fulfilling daily orders, counting inventory, loading, and unloading trucks, moving products, and processing returns safely. This role will require the training in and use of PIT equipment, specifically the order picker (reach truck), cherry picker and forklift. The rewards of the position are high visibility and a positive impact on literacy worldwide. Growth opportunities exist as we are a startup and anticipate robust growth in business year over year.

To be equitable to all Literatians, we have set compensation and a non-negotiation policy- we don't think a person’s pay should be determined by how comfortable they negotiate. As a result, we like to be transparent and up-front about the compensation. The hourly rate for this role will be $17.50/hr.

Shifts available between Monday-Friday 7:00 am - 3:30 pm

Part-Time

ABOUT YOU

 - High School Graduate/GED

 - Experienced on PIT equipment (Order picker, reach, forklift)

 - Detail Oriented

 - Highly Organized

 - Able to Count and Speak English

 - Strong Work Ethic

 - Ability to walk and stand for long periods of time

WE’D LOVE TO SEE

 - High-Quality Standards

 - Strong Communication

 - Task-Oriented

 - Committed to Excellence

ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Picking, Packing, Order Fulfillment, Counting, Cycle Counts, Advertised Title Walks, QA, Transfers, Fair Production, Auditing, and Training.

 - Mobility and Standing: This position requires the ability to remain standing and moving throughout the entirety of the shift. Candidates should be comfortable being on their feet for extended periods of time to successfully perform job duties.

 - Lifting Requirements: The role involves occasional lifting of items weighing up to 50 pounds. Employees may be required to safely lift, carry, and maneuver items within this weight range as part of their regular duties.

 - Flexibility and Dexterity: Regular activities include bending, twisting, squatting, and reaching. These movements are integral to performing tasks associated with the role, such as handling materials, operating machinery, or completing manual tasks.

Disclaimer: This job description is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities, or qualifications associated with the job. The employer reserves the right to modify this job description at any time.

We are dedicated to cultivating and preserving a culture of inclusion and connectedness that sees, reflects, welcomes, and celebrates the innovation and talent of a diverse array of people. We seek people with drive and a touch of alchemy. We also recognize that some people — particularly underrepresented groups, women, BIPOC, LGBTQ+, etc. — may not be comfortable applying to a position unless they meet 100% of the qualifications. Even if your experience isn’t a precise match for the role, we highly encourage you to apply. And if your career has taken you to some spectacular — or spectacularly strange — places, we love a good story.

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Senior Full Stack Engineer



Think of your favorite book from childhood. Got it? Great. Now, that warm, joyful feeling you have right now—that’s what we do. Literati is a modern children’s book company focused on helping kids fall in love with reading at every age and stage. We make it easy to find great books through our school book fairs, school store fundraising platform, and book clubs using a unique mix of expert curation and data science. We’re on a mission to help parents ignite a love of learning and a love of life.



Role Overview

As a Senior Full Stack Engineer, you will support our consumer-facing systems for the school store, kids club products, and new school fundraising initiatives. In particular we are looking for someone who is a strong front end developer who is also able to make contributions with Python as needed. You will play a crucial role in designing and building engaging user experiences to drive customer growth and retention. You'll be an essential part of the team working to improve children's literacy across the United States. As part of a small team, you'll have many opportunities for personal and career growth while collaborating with industry experts in machine learning and personalization. The salary band for this role is $140,000 - $150,000, depending on experience.



REQUIREMENTS

 - 4+ years of experience defining and leading web application server architecture initiatives

 - 3+ years of experience building web applications using React

 - Expertise in API and Data Model design

 - Proficiency with Front End technologies, including CSS and JavaScript frameworks

 - Proficiency with Python

 - Strong communication skills to convey architectural perspectives and monitor implementations

 - Experience developing tests that ensure performance, quality, and scalability

 - Proven success delivering in an agile environment

 - Proficiency with tools such as Git, Shortcut/Jira, etc.

 - Demonstrated leadership in engineering environments through operational excellence, best practices, and mentoring junior developers



WE’D LOVE TO SEE

 - Experience with Literati’s full tech stack (Python/Django, React, Kubernetes, etc.)

 - Ability to work with the product team and customers to define requirements to build out new features.

 - Experience implementing and documenting engineering processes and standards;



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the full software development lifecycle from concept to deployment, collaborating with cross-functional teams to plan, design, and deliver robust and scalable solutions.

 - Mentor and elevate the team's technical skills by conducting thorough and constructive code reviews, ensuring code quality, maintainability, and adherence to established best practices.

 - Champion code quality and reliability by implementing comprehensive testing strategies, including automated unit, integration, and end-to-end tests, and ensuring proper QA coverage.

 - Drive technical excellence by leading initiatives to enhance software development processes. This includes improving system monitoring, logging, and proactively managing technical debt to ensure long-term health and stability of our codebases.



ABOUT LITERATI

We offer competitive salaries, company equity, medical/dental/vision insurance, 401k, short-term disability insurance, parental leave, employee assistance programs, flexible work schedules, and flexible time off.

We are dedicated to cultivating and preserving a culture of inclusion and connectedness that sees, reflects, welcomes, and celebrates the innovation and talent of a diverse array of people. We seek people with drive and a touch of alchemy. We also recognize that some people—particularly underrepresented groups, women, BIPOC, and LGBTQ+—may not be comfortable applying to a position unless they meet 100% of the qualifications. Even if your experience isn’t a precise match for the role, we highly encourage you to apply. And if your career has taken you to some

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative (BDR)
source_jobs.description:
Business Development Representative (BDR)

About the BDR Role at Literati

As a Business Development Representative focused on bringing exceptional book fairs to schools, you'll be at the heart of our mission to introduce Literati Book Fairs to elementary schools. In this pivotal role, you'll partner with schools to enhance their fundraising efforts while championing the transformative power of reading and literacy for children. This role is responsible for achieving quarterly/annual fair count and market penetration goals.

Your primary responsibility will be connecting with schools through strategic outbound calling, nurturing inbound leads via phone and email, and ultimately securing multiple seasons of scheduled book fair events. This sales-driven position centers on building meaningful relationships with educators and administrators who share our passion for student success.

The ideal candidate brings genuine enthusiasm for promoting literacy in schools, coupled with a strong background in sales and territory management abilities, and exceptional motivation. We're looking for someone with a collaborative mindset and an upbeat, engaging personality who thrives in a goal-oriented environment.

This role may have opportunities to represent Literati Book Fairs at trade shows and conferences, sharing our story and expanding our reach within the education community. Success in this role means consistently achieving monthly, seasonal, and annual targets while strategically scheduling events to maximize impact. Starting rate $22.67/hour



Responsibilities

 - Achieve quarterly/annual book fair booking quotas and territory growth goals

 - Execute strategic outbound calling campaigns

 - Drive territory market penetration and growth

 - Develop and implement comprehensive territory plans

 - Deliver compelling district and school presentations

 - Maintain accurate CRM documentation

 - Compete effectively in the marketplace

 - Other duties as assigned, you love the idea of championing literacy while driving measurable results, we want to hear from you.

What We Look For

 - Minimum 3+ years of account or territory management experience

 - Exceptional verbal and written communication abilities

 - Successful outbound cold calling background

 - Consistent history of meeting or exceeding sales targets

 - Experience working with educational institutions is preferred

 - Mac computer proficiency is advantageous

 - Reliable high-speed home internet connection required

We are dedicated to cultivating and preserving a culture of inclusion and connectedness that sees, reflects, welcomes, and celebrates the innovation and talent of a diverse array of people. We seek people with drive and a touch of alchemy. We also recognize that some people—particularly underrepresented groups, women, BIPOC, and LGBTQ+—may not be comfortable applying to a position unless they meet 100% of the qualifications. Even if your experience isn't a precise match for the role, we highly encourage you to apply. And if your career has taken you to some spectacular (or spectacularly strange) places, we love a good story.

source_jobs.title: Events Operations Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Events Operations Coordinator



Think of your favorite book from childhood. Got it? Great. Now, that warm, joyful feeling you have right now—that’s what we do. Literati is a modern children’s book company focused on helping kids fall in love with reading at every age and stage. We make it easy to find great books through our school book fairs, school store fundraising platform, and book clubs using a unique mix of expert curation and data science. We’re on a mission to help parents ignite a love of learning and a love of life.

As an Events Operations Coordinator for Literati, your role is to help scale our national conference and event operations while ensuring a consistent, professional, and engaging brand experience across every touchpoint.

In this role, you will primarily support conference planning, logistics, and operational infrastructure while partnering closely with Sales, Marketing, Creative, and Operations teams to coordinate event assets, inventory, and post-event follow-up. You’ll help build scalable systems and repeatable processes that support both brand growth and revenue generation.

This role is based in the greater Chicagoland area (Chicago or surrounding suburbs) and requires occasional travel to our Elgin office (approximately 3–4 times per year) and national conferences (approximately 3–4 trips per year), which may include evenings and weekends.

The salary band for this role is $50,000–$60,000 annually. This position is classified as exempt and is not eligible for overtime pay.



RESPONSIBILITIES

Conference Operations & Execution

 - Own conference calendar management, registrations, exhibitor deadlines, logistics, and booth execution

 - Coordinate conference materials, sponsorship deliverables, staffing logistics, shipping, and travel communication

 - Partner with Operations to ensure conference assets and materials are prepared, shipped, and event-ready

 - Support post-event reporting, lead handoff, and conference debrief processes

Operational Infrastructure & Cross-Functional Coordination

 - Build and maintain conference playbooks, timelines, inventory tracking, and operational processes

 - Standardize conference booth packages and materials to support scalable event execution

 - Partner with Sales, Marketing, Creative, and Operations teams to coordinate conference execution and materials

 - Create and manage creative briefs while tracking timelines, approvals, and delivery of conference assets

 - Monitor post-event lead follow-up activity and flag gaps in sales engagement

 - Maintain clean and accurate data across event and lead management workflows

Additional Team Support

 - Support webinar logistics, lead queue coverage, incentive fulfillment, and occasional community engagement needs

 - Become fully trained to support conference attendance and onsite execution when needed, independently

 - Other duties as assigned

Success in this role looks like

 - Conferences and events are executed proactively, with deadlines met

 - Conference materials, inventory, and logistics are organized, scalable, and consistently event-ready

 - Cross-functional workflows run smoothly, supporting efficient event execution and post-event follow-up

REQUIREMENTS

 - 2+ years of experience in event coordination, conference support, marketing operations, sales support, project coordination, or related fields

 - Strong organizational and project management skills with exceptional attention to detail

 - Experience coordinating multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Ability to collaborate cross-functionally in a fast-paced environment

 - Experience with Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and CRM systems such as Salesforce is preferred

 - Ability to travel occasionally for conferences and events

ABOUT LITERATI

We offer competitive salaries, medical/dental/vision insurance, 401k, short-term disability insurance, parental leave, employee a

source_jobs.title: Dock Specialist
source_jobs.description:
At Literati, we believe in the power of terrific books. We are on a mission to ignite a love of learning and a love of life by curating transformative literary experiences for every reader: anyone who dreams, anyone who wonders, of any age. There are some stories no child should live without, and no adult should ever forget.



Literati is the fastest-growing company in Arts + Literature in the United States and the fifth fastest growing company in Texas. After launching an innovative try-before-you-buy print book club for kids in 2017, the company expanded in 2020 into hosting print and digital book clubs for the world's top authors, celebrities, and brands. New for 2022, Literati has expanded into children’s book fairs. Lovingly referred to as Austin's "fiction factory," the company curates and fulfills over a million books each quarter.



As a Dock Specialist you will report to the Fulfillment Center Leadership focused on ensuring we utilize our dock space fully and ship and receive product accurately. You will play a crucial role in inventory accuracy, quality control, and dock management. The Dock Specialist responsibilities primarily include but are not limited to: driver check-in, loading and unloading bookfair shipments, managing BOLs and quality documentation for truckloads, updating systems as shipments occur, unloading and receiving of new inventory, general dock flow and organization. The impact areas for the Dock Specialist are revenue growth, Fulfillment Center execution, and quality control. The rewards of the position are high visibility and positive impact on literacy world-wide. Growth opportunities exist as we are a startup and anticipate robust growth in business year over year.

To be equitable to all Literatians, we have set compensation and a non-negotiation policy- we don't think a person’s pay should be determined by how comfortable they negotiate. As a result, we like to be transparent and up-front about the compensation. When we make an offer, we can provide options so employees can choose between compensation packages, prioritizing salary or equity. The hourly rate for this role will be $18.00/hr.



WE’RE GOOD AT:
Bringing the fire. We are a startup, with all the fierce dedication and sparkling energy to accomplish great feats. Iron sharpens iron, and so we have refined our teams from only the strongest metals: open minds, bright ideas, and bold determination.



YOU’RE GOOD AT:

 - Equipment Operation (minimum sit-down required)

 - Safety

 - High Quality Standards

 - Strong Math Skills

 - Highly Organized

 - Detail Oriented

 - Tech Savvy

 - Interpreting & Executing Plan

 - Communication & Cooperation

 - Committed to Excellence





YOU WILL NEED:

 - High School Graduate / GED

 - Lift Equipment (Sit Down/Standard) Experience

 - Strong Communication

 - Attention to Detail

 - Task Oriented

 - Ability to walk and stand for prolonged periods of time

 - Ability to lift up to 50lbs

 - Requires frequent bending, lifting and moving



YOUR DAY-TO-DAY WILL LOOK LIKE:

 - Freight Handling: Load and unload cargo safely by hand or using forklifts, hand trucks, or pallet jacks when appropriate. Stack, secure, and wrap freight to prevent damage during transit.

 - Documentation & Accuracy: Verify shipping manifests, bills of lading, and delivery receipts. Accurately input data into warehouse inventory systems.

 - Quality Control: Inspect arriving and departing shipments for damage, missing items, or discrepancies, and report them to supervisors.

 - Teamwork: Communicate frequently with supervisors, collaborate with other team members, and assist in other areas as needed.



We seek people with drive and a touch of alchemy—and if that sounds like you, you should join us. Even if your experience is not a precise match for the role, grit, passion, and prowess will always win the day in our book. And if your career has taken you to some spectacular (or spectacularly strange) places? We love a goo

source_jobs.title: Seasonal Full-Time BF Support Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LITERATI

Think of your favorite book from childhood. Got it? Great. Now, that warm, joyful feeling you have right now—that’s what we do. Literati is a modern children’s book company focused on building reading skills so kids can grow to be their best selves. We make it easy to find books kids love through school book fairs and book clubs using a unique mix of expert curation and data science. We’re on a mission to help parents ignite a love of learning and a love of life.

 

If you’re looking for meaningful work that fits into real life, this role was designed with you in mind. As a Seasonal Full-Time Support Associate, you’ll help school communities run joyful book fairs filled with child-like wonder—while working a predictable schedule that allows you to step away at the end of the day to focus on what matters most outside of work.


This is a seasonal, time-bound role designed to support Book Fair coordinators during our busiest seasons. You’ll provide exceptional customer service via phone and email, help facilitate book orders for schools, and coordinate with other team members to ensure each school is fully taken care of. Seasonal Full-Time Support Associates will focus on live queue coverage and standard, documented workflows.This role does not oversee long-term project ownership, escalation management, or cross-functional program leadership, which remain the responsibility of permanent Full-Time employees.


Hours: This is a seasonal position with a fixed end date. This position will be scheduled 25-35 hours per week from approximately August 31st to November 20, 2026. Overtime is not permitted. Schedules may vary week to week to support peak coverage needs. Operating hours for this department are from 9am - 9pm CST. Employment in this role ends at the conclusion of the season. Any future seasonal opportunities are discretionary and not guaranteed.

 

To be equitable to all Literatians, we have set compensation and a non-negotiation policy—we don't think a person’s pay should be determined by how comfortably they negotiate. As a result, we like to be transparent and up-front about the compensation. The hourly rate for this role is $17.50 per hour. As a seasonal employee, this position is not eligible for standard employee benefits, except those required by applicable federal, state, and local law.

 

ABOUT YOU

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

 - Comfortable providing support via phone and email

 - Ability to work independently and as part of a team

 - Strong attention to detail and accuracy

 - Ability to prioritize, multitask and stay organized

 - Comfortable following documented processes and workflows

 - Proficient in using resources to provide great customer service

 

WE’D LOVE TO SEE

 - Experience answering phones and emails

 - Strong organizational skills

 - Flexibility to change tasks throughout the day

 

ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Answering customer inquiries via phone and email

 - Providing personal customer service to the contact running the book fair at the school

 - Checking in with the contact at the school to help run their fair

 - Distributing resources to the book fair contact to assist in book fair planning and operation

 - Providing internal support for Business Development Reps and Account Managers

 - Using Google Sheets, Slack and Salesforce

 - Facilitating customer requests to ensure the fairs stay stocked through their duration

 - Updating the CRM as customer inquiries are solved

 - Operating and helping instruct customers on how to use the Square register

At Literati, our employees are our greatest asset, and we strive to create a culture that empowers, inspires, and supports them in achieving their full potential. As an employee of Literati, you will:

 1. Be part of a mission-driven company that is making a real difference in children’s lives by igniting a love of learning and a love of life.

 2. Work alongside some of the brightest, most creative, an

notes: Literati is a modern children's book company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
